title: Coverage of essential health services
subtitle: Coverage of essential health services is measured as an index on a scale of 0 to 100 (where higher is better). This index is based on risk-standardized death rates – which give a measure of healthcare access and quality – and whether common interventions are carried out.
caption:
Coverage of essential health services, 2023

Coverage of essential health services is measured as an index on a scale of 0 to 100 (where higher is better). This index is based on risk-standardized death rates – which give a measure of healthcare access and quality – and whether common interventions are carried out.

The choropleth map displays the 2023 coverage of essential health services across nations, using a continuous color scale where darker blues represent higher index scores and lighter greens represent lower scores. The healthcare index used to assess coverage is based on a range of health quality and access indicators and risk-standardized death rates which give a measure of healthcare access and quality, acknowledging that countries provide a wide range of services for health promotion, prevention, treatment, and care, including rehabilitation and palliation. The highest values, falling in the dark blue band near 100, are visible across North America, Western Europe, Australia, and parts of South America including Brazil and Argentina. In contrast, the lowest values appear in the light green band between 0 and 20, concentrated in the Sahel region of Africa, including Chad, Niger, and Mali, as well as parts of Central Africa. Intermediate shades of teal and medium blue cover much of Asia, Eastern Europe, and the Middle East, with countries like India and China falling in the 40 to 60 range. Greenland and a few other territories are marked with hatching to indicate no data is available. The geographic distribution shows a clear gradient where high-income regions in the northern hemisphere and Oceania cluster at the top of the scale, while low-income regions in sub-Saharan Africa cluster at the bottom.
